title: Two-Finger scroll defaults to config panel instead of remote desktop
author: rbyrnedugan
body_snippet:
<!--
Only file GitHub issues for bugs and feature requests.  All other topics will be closed.

Before opening an issue, please search for a duplicate or closed issue.
Please provide as much detail as possible for us to fix your issue.
-->

<!-- Bug -->
|||
|--:|---|
|Operating system | Windows 10 x64 |
|mRemoteNG version| 1.75.7008.17907  |

Whenever I have the connections and config panels pinned to the side, my two-finger scroll (on the touchpad) will scroll the config panel inste...

iterations.description: Issue body is empty with no reproducible steps, and the triage comment already requested retest/details with no substantive response from the reporter.

comments.author: penderes
comments.snippet: I have the same problem. I was not able to find a way to get around this yet.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage: Issue body is empty with no reproducible steps, and the triage comment already requested retest/details with no substantive response from the reporter.
Approach: If confirmed, investigate WinForms mouse wheel message routing to ensure scroll events reach the active RDP/remote panel instead of the config panel when the cursor is over the remote desktop area.
